name: Rowan Ironhand
voicePatterns: few words, chosen carefully — lets his work speak
short declarative sentences about craft and materials
observant comments about things others miss
speaks with the rhythm of someone used to hammer-strikes
dialogueHooks: offering to repair or improve equipment
commenting on the quality of metal and materials
noting that his forge fire has been burning differently since the Thinning started
quietly observing village tensions without taking sides

always: state of Thornwick's tools, weapons, and hardware
which materials are getting harder to source
the forge has been in his family for three generations
sometimes: his forge fire follows Threadlines — it burns hotter near ward-circles
certain metals behave differently near thin places
travelers from Loomhearth mentioned Wardweaver tool orders increasing
rarely: he's noticed that iron worked during the Binding Song ceremonies has subtly different properties
he keeps samples of this 'ceremony iron' but has told no one

morning.activity: stoking the forge, starting the day's work

evening.activity: quiet drink, listening more than talking

backstory: Rowan's family has run the Thornwick forge for three generations. He's a craftsman, not a talker. He observes everything and says little. His forge is the practical backbone of the village — without it, nothing gets fixed, no tools get made, and Thornwick slowly falls apart. He's noticed things about his craft that connect to the weave in ways he doesn't fully understand.
secret: Iron worked during Binding Song ceremonies has subtly different properties — stronger, more resonant. Rowan keeps samples of this 'ceremony iron' and has never told anyone. This is an early hint of Thread-Iron crafting that Wren Looseweft will pioneer in Act II.
motivation: Keep the forge running and Thornwick's tools in working order
tensions: notices magical properties in his craft but identifies as purely practical
quiet observer who sees tensions others miss but rarely intervenes

knownFacts: Bryn always pays her forge bills on time
bryn_oakheart.history: Professional respect. Rowan admires Bryn's work ethic.
